name: Night of the Radishes
traditions: Carved radish sculptures depicting nativity scenes and historical events; competition for best carving; market stalls; buñuelos.
historicalBackground: Noche de Rábanos began in 1897 when Oaxacan merchants carved radishes to attract customers at the Christmas market. It became an annual competition.
description: Oaxaca's wonderfully quirky Christmas tradition, where artisans compete to create the most elaborate sculptures carved from giant radishes.
